questions.statement: The main method is the place where execution begins.
questions.explanation: Execution begins in the main method.

questions.statement: The file name can be different from the class name.
questions.explanation: The file name should match the class name.

questions.statement: Every Java statement ends with a semicolon.
questions.explanation: A semicolon marks the end of the statement.

questions.statement: System.out.print() moves to the next line after printing.
questions.explanation: print stays on the same line.

questions.statement: System.out.println() moves to the next line after printing.
questions.explanation: println starts a new line after printing.

questions.statement: String is used to store text.
questions.explanation: String stores text values.

questions.statement: byte, short, and int are used for whole numbers.
questions.explanation: These are integral data types.

questions.statement: double is used for decimal numbers.
questions.explanation: double stores decimal values.

questions.statement: A variable is a named storage location.
questions.explanation: A variable stores a value using a name.

questions.statement: Initialization means assigning a value to a variable.
questions.explanation: Initialization gives the variable its value.

questions.statement: The modulus operator gives the remainder after division.
questions.explanation: The symbol % gives the remainder.

questions.statement: The operator + is an arithmetic operator.
questions.explanation: It is used for addition.

questions.statement: An operand is the symbol that performs the action.
questions.explanation: The operator performs the action. The operands are the values.

questions.statement: An expression produces a value when evaluated.
questions.explanation: That is the meaning of an expression.

questions.statement: A statement is a complete command in Java.
questions.explanation: A statement tells Java to perform an action.

questions.statement: The symbol = is the simple assignment operator.
questions.explanation: It assigns the right side value to the left side variable.

questions.statement: A binary operator needs two operands.
questions.explanation: One operand is on each side.

questions.statement: In 100 + 800, the operands are 100 and 800.
questions.explanation: The values on both sides of + are the operands.

questions.statement: Curly brackets are used to define code blocks.
questions.explanation: They show where blocks begin and end.

questions.statement: Main and main are the same in Java.
questions.explanation: Java is case-sensitive.

questions.statement: float and double are both used for decimal values.
questions.explanation: Both are decimal data types.

questions.statement: byte has the range -128 to 127.
questions.explanation: That is the byte range in the portion.

questions.statement: short uses less memory than byte.
questions.explanation: byte uses less memory than short.

questions.statement: int age = 13; shows declaration and initialization together.
questions.explanation: The variable is declared and given a value.

questions.statement: String city = Chennai; is correct Java code.
questions.explanation: A String literal must be inside double quotes.

questions.statement: System.out.println(100 + 800); is a statement.
questions.explanation: It is a complete command ending with a semicolon.

questions.statement: The operator in 45 / 9 is /.
questions.explanation: The division sign is the operator.

questions.statement: The expression 12 + 8 gives 20.
questions.explanation: 12 plus 8 equals 20.

questions.statement: The main method is written inside the class block.
questions.explanation: It appears inside the class declaration.

questions.statement: The assignment operator compares two values.
questions.explanation: The simple assignment operator stores a value in a variable.
